Empires & Puzzles D'Andre Review: The 4-Star Rush War Specialist

Step right up! We're reviewing D'Andre, the magician who turns defense down into defense up. Despite aging stats, his mana buff makes him a Rush War staple. Does he still have enough magic to compete with modern 4-stars? Let's find out.
D'Andre, The Rush War Ringmaster: Turning Debuffs into Buffs with Style

D'Andre is an Epic (4-Star) Holy healer originally from the Starfall Circus event, now residing in the Challenge Festival II portal. Back in his debut, he was hailed as a mini-Director Zuri or a "Yellow Raphael" for 4-star tournaments. His kit is a classic "support trifecta": healing, a defensive flip, and the ever-coveted mana generation buff. While he doesn't hit enemies with a hammer like some modern 4-stars, he keeps your team alive and firing faster, which is often more valuable. He is the guy you bring when you want your slow heroes to feel fast.

D'Andre sports a look that screams "eccentric street magician meets circus ringmaster." With his top hat, purple suspenders, and juggling clubs, he definitely stands out from the usual crowd of armored knights and angry barbarians. The art style captures a bit of that mischievous Starfall energy. He looks like he’s about to ask you to pick a card, any card, and then somehow pull a rabbit out of your defense team.
When we look at D'Andre's base stats (676 Power), the age starts to show. Modern 4-star heroes like

are pushing 800 Power out of the box, with base stats that rival older 5-stars. D'Andre's defense (637) and health (1381) are on the squishy side by 2026 standards. Without Limit Breaking and emblems, he is liable to vanish faster than a magician's assistant in a box. However, his Paladin class gives him a chance to boost that defense, which is a saving grace.
This skill is the definition of "utility."
It's a solid, immediate heal. Not an overheat, but reliable.
This is his party trick. If an ally has a defense down ailment (hello,
), D'Andre doesn't just cleanse it; he flips it into a +50% Defense buff. This turns a vulnerability into a fortress.
The +24% mana generation is huge. It helps your average heroes charge in 9 tiles instead of 10 (with the right troops), and makes slow heroes bearable.
Average. He needs 10 tiles to fire.
At max levels (roughly 1400 HP), a 35% heal is about
. With Limit Break 2 (approx 1740 HP), that jumps to roughly
require 10 tiles. With D'Andre's buff, they gain mana effectively at 1.24x speed. 10 / 1.24 = ~8.06. This means after D'Andre fires, your other Average heroes effectively become Fast.
In Rush wars, everyone is Very Fast (6.5 tiles). The mana buff is less critical for the speed break there, but it counters mana slows from enemies (like Sorcerer troops or ailment stacks).
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Starfall family bonus is one of the best in the game for long fights. Having a 40-60% chance to cleanse the most recent ailment
at the end of every turn
is incredible passive mitigation. It can shrug off burns, poisons, or defense downs without you lifting a finger.
D'Andre relies purely on his Family Bonus; he does not have modern passive skills like "start battle with mana" or "resist blocks."
gives him a defense boost against Special Skills for the first few turns. This is actually perfect for him, as it helps him survive the initial barrage of snipes so he can fire his heal.

Upon release, D'Andre was a revelation for 4-star tournaments and wars. He was a premium healer in yellow, a color that often lacked good 4-star healing options (sorry, Lady Woolerton and your mana block).
His stats have fallen behind significantly. He is squishy. However, his
have not aged. A 35% heal + Mana Gen is a combination that will always be useful. The "defense flip" remains a hard counter to ubiquitous heroes like Treevil in 4-star Rush formats.

He is no longer a tank option, but as a flank or wing in 4-star tournaments (especially Rush or Bloody Battle where he cleanses/buffs even if he can't heal), he still sees play. In 5-star wars, he is risky unless fully Limit Broken.
D'Andre currently does not have a costume. What you see is what you get!
D'Andre's job is to stay alive long enough to heal the team. He does zero damage with his special. Therefore, we ignore attack completely.
Maximizes his survivability. The Paladin class talent (Protect) scales off defense, making him sturdier.
His tile damage will be wet noodle status.
. There is no scenario where you want D'Andre to have higher attack power at the cost of survivability.
+24% mana gen makes the whole team run smoother.
Specifically counters the defense-down aspect of the 4-star Rush king, Treevil.
The Starfall family bonus is a lifesaver in attrition battles.
Fills a crucial role in 4-star rarity where good Holy healers are scarce.
** outdated Stats:** Low defense and HP make him a prime target for modern snipers.
In a non-Rush setting, 10 tiles can feel like an eternity if the board is bad.
The defense flip only works if the team actually has a defense ailment. Otherwise, it does nothing extra.
This is his playground. He speeds up allies and counters defense down ailments.
He provides two buffs (Defense conditional, Mana Gen) to the whole team.
The mana generation helps offset the curses or speed up the team to clear floors faster.
D'Andre keeps the team healthy and speeds up the slow monsters like Franz and Colen. If the enemy brings a defense down hero, D'Andre punishes them.
Guardian Jackal - D'Andre - Chao - Joon - Mist
Jackal applies elemental defense down, D'Andre speeds everyone up, and Mist slows the enemy down. It creates a massive speed gap between you and the enemy.
who block buffs prevent his mana generation and defense flip.
if you rely on her heal first, as he cannot cleanse her self-inflicted mana block (it's undispellable mostly, but check your clerical interactions).

If you pull D'Andre early, rejoice! He is a fantastic healer who will carry you through the map stages. The mana generation is a "hidden" power that makes difficult levels easier by letting your damage dealers fire more often. Prioritize leveling him over basic Season 1 healers like Vivica (who is slow).

You likely have him sitting on your bench. Is he worth the Limit Break 2? Probably not for general use, given the stats of new constructs and gargoyles. However, if you take 4-star tournaments seriously, specifically Rush Attack, he remains a top-tier support. If you struggle against Treevil tanks in 4-star wars, dust him off.
The community has always had a soft spot for the "pimp magician."
Back in the day, players noted his synergy with other "circus freaks." One player mentioned,
"Even at this level of play, people are still using a lot of Treevils... luckily, D'Andre can help us overcome this problem."
Another common sentiment was comparing him to 5-star healers:
"He's basically a 4-star Ariel without the cleanse but with a defense buff."
The "defense flip" mechanic is often cited as one of the most satisfying moves in the game:
"There is nothing better than turning a -44% defense down into a +50% fortress and watching the enemy hit for zero."

D'Andre is a classic example of "Skills pay the bills." His stats are rusty, his outfit is loud, but his kit is timeless. In the specific niche of 4-star Rush wars and tournaments, he is still a VIP guest. He brings healing, speed, and a nasty surprise for anyone relying on defense down. While you might not take him to a top 500 Alliance War anymore, he’s definitely the star of the show in the Epic arena.
Keep him, emblem him for defense, and let him juggle your team to victory in 4-star tournaments.
A deep dive into D'Andre, the Holy 4-Star healer from the Starfall Circus family. Is his mana generation buff enough to keep him relevant in the modern meta?
